Singtel Group is Asia's leading communications technology group, providing a portfolio of services from next-generation communication, digital services and digital infrastructure including regional data centre arm Nxera and regional IT services arm NCS. The Group has presence in Asia, Australia and Africa and reaches over 820 million mobile customers in 20 countries. 

Singtel is dedicated to continuous innovation, harnessing next-generation technologies to create new and exciting customer experiences as we shape a more sustainable, digital future.

According to the IMDA Singapore Digital Economy Report 2025, only 14.5% of SMEs have implemented AI solutions, compared to 62.5% of larger enterprises — highlighting a widening competitive gap. For many SMEs, the challenge isn’t recognising AI’s potential but knowing where to start and how to implement it effectively.
 
To help bridge this, Singtel Singapore launched AI.dea, a new AI business transformation programme developed in partnership with Singapore Institute of Management (SIM) Academy. AI.dea is designed to support SMEs in moving from experimentation to implementation through a structured, hands-on approach. Participants will learn how to design, build and validate AI use cases tailored to their needs, while strengthening capabilities in governance, security and sustainable adoption. 
 
To further support adoption, eligible SMEs can also receive up to 90% funding support from SkillsFuture SG.
 
Ms Jasmin Lau, Minister of State, Ministry of Digital Development and Information joined the launch event, and discussed the various factors to consider when adopting AI during a fireside chat with Singtel Singapore CEO, Tian Chong Ng and SIM Academy CEO Seong Kim Ho. 

Singtel Singapore CEO Ng Tian Chong said, “AI has quickly become a key driver of business transformation, but many organisations are still figuring out where to even begin. Singtel Singapore’s own journey has shown that success with AI requires more than just technology – it takes the right capabilities, governance and mindset. With AI.dea, we are bringing these lessons to SMEs, helping them to adopt AI applications before committing to full-scale deployment so they can realise tangible business outcomes.”
 
SMEs that are keen to unlock new opportunities for productivity, innovation and growth with AI can sign up here: https://lnkd.in/gjTzM-NJ
 
More info on the announcement: https://lnkd.in/gPxu8WSC
 
#AI #Innovation #SMEs #Enterprises

As sovereign AI initiatives accelerate across the region, enterprises need infrastructure that is not just powerful — but secure, sovereign and built for their sector.

Today, we announced a strategic partnership between Singtel Digital InfraCo's sovereign AI cloud RE:AI (Singtel) and European AI leader Mistral AI, to co-develop AI solutions and establish a joint Applied AI Centre of Excellence (CoE) in Singapore.

The partnership combines Mistral AI's open-source stack - including foundation models, training capabilities and agentic workflows - with RE:AI's GPU infrastructure to deliver scalable AI-as-a-Service designed for essential industries and public sector such as financial services, defence, government and healthcare. 

The joint Applied AI CoE will serve as an engineering hub where teams from both companies co-create, prototype and deploy sector-specific AI solutions - with Singtel Singapore as the pilot customer, piloting use cases including customer care AI chatbots, network operations copilots and AI-assisted network tender evaluation. Government agencies and enterprise partners will follow as early adopters.

Singtel Digital InfraCo CEO Bill Chang said, "As sovereign AI initiatives accelerate across the public and private sectors in the region, our digital infrastructure and AI cloud provide the secure, scalable environment needed to drive adoption and unlock greater innovation. Collaborating with AI leaders like Mistral AI furthers our mission to make AI easily accessible, giving our customers flexibility and choice while meeting their data governance and security requirements. Enhancing our sovereign AI capabilities will also reinforce Singapore's position as a trusted hub for digital growth and AI innovation."

Mistral AI’s commitment to open-source development, combined with RE:AI’s provision of affordable AI access without heavy infrastructure overheads, will make high-performance computing more widely available and help enterprises to embrace AI with scale and speed. 
 
This partnership also deepens Singtel Digital InfraCo's AI ecosystem, with Mistral AI contributing resources where applicable to our CoE for Applied AI with NVIDIA.

Read more here: https://lnkd.in/gh3JGDaA

#SingtelDigitalInfraCo #REAI #MistralAI #SovereignAI #Singapore #NationalAIStrategy #opensourcestack

Singtel Group has launched an exercise to transfer Singtel Special Discounted Shares (SDS) from the CPF Board to The Central Depository (CDP) accounts of SDS holders. This initiative benefits SDS holders by giving them greater ease and control in managing their holdings and exercise their rights as shareholders directly. 

Introduced in the 1990s, the SDS scheme was part of the government’s efforts to give Singaporeans a stake in Singapore’s economic success through share ownership. Many Singaporeans bought ST “A” shares in 1993 or ST2 shares in 1996 with their CPF savings and the CPF Board was appointed to facilitate the purchase as Singaporeans were new to share ownership at that time. Today, as Singaporeans are more knowledgeable and familiar with share ownership, the SDS scheme has met its intent and the legacy arrangement through CPF Board is no longer needed.

The transfer will enable SDS holders who hold shares in their individual CDP accounts to consolidate all their holdings, making them easier to track and trade. As Singtel shareholders, SDS holders will also benefit, as Singtel will have greater flexibility to carry out corporate actions in a timely and cost-efficient manner. This initiative will give Singtel more options to reward shareholders and fund growth initiatives. 

The transfer of Singtel SDS is scheduled on 21 November 2026, subject to legislative approval.

Read more here: https://lnkd.in/gTiUdi4t

We are proud to announce the launch of Singtel Innov8’s new US$250 million AI Growth Fund – dedicated to investing in high-growth AI startups globally and accelerating applied AI adoption across the Singtel Group. This isn't just a financial commitment. It deepens our role across the full AI value chain – as an adopter deploying AI across our own operations, an enabler powering businesses and governments, and a provider delivering AI-driven connectivity and digital infrastructure at scale.

With this fund, Singtel Innov8 will create a pipeline of deployable AI innovation across Singtel Group’s operating companies. 

Edgar Hardless, CEO of Singtel Innov8, said, “Singtel Innov8’s AI Growth Fund gives Singtel Group strategic access to transformative AI technologies at a pivotal stage of growth. It also enables us to test, integrate and scale AI innovations across our networks, platforms and digital infrastructure. By investing with clear deployment pathways, we accelerate applied AI adoption for enterprises, governments and consumers across the region. This initiative underscores the Group's mission of empowering enterprises, governments and consumers with world-class digital and AI capabilities, reinforcing Singapore's and the region's position as a global hub for responsible and cutting-edge AI innovation."

With more than fifteen years of corporate venture investment experience, Innov8 is one of Southeast Asia’s earliest and most active corporate venture capital firms, with over 120 investments globally. It connects startups with cutting edge technologies from global innovation hubs with Singtel Group to drive long-term strategic value. 

Read more here: https://lnkd.in/g5hinWgC

#Singtel #Innov8 #ArtificialIntelligence #ST28 #Innovation #AIGrowthFund

Enterprises have long treated backup data as a safeguard for recovery, but it holds far greater untapped value. Today, we announced Singtel Digital InfraCo’s sovereign AI cloud RE:AI (Singtel)’s partnership with Cohesity, to launch an intelligent, sovereign AI data security and management service in Singapore.
 
Many enterprises have years’ worth of important information such as old documents, emails, reports and system records stored away in backup systems. They are difficult for today’s large language models and AI applications to search or learn from as these AI tools are built to work only with current active data. The new offering from RE:AI and Cohesity empowers enterprises and government agencies to catalogue and organise vast amounts of historical data so it can be searched and queried in real time. This will help them to generate more accurate, data-driven responses from richer, longer-range context.
 
Singtel Digital InfraCo CEO Bill Chang said, "By combining RE:AI's sovereign AI capabilities with market-leading data security capabilities from Cohesity, enterprises can finally harness historical data that has long remained untapped to generate deeper insights and make faster, more informed decisions. Importantly, they can do this while keeping sensitive information protected and within Singapore’s regulatory environment."
 
This partnership also deepens Singtel Digital InfraCo’s AI ecosystem, with Cohesity contributing R&D resources to our new Centre of Excellence for Applied AI with NVIDIA. 
 
Read more here: https://lnkd.in/gFbgbnjY
 
#Singtel #REAI #AI #Innovation #Transformation #Cloud

From AI promise to realising AI’s potential – today, Singtel Digital InfraCo and NVIDIA announced the launch of a Centre of Excellence for Applied AI in Singapore at an event graced by Guest of Honour Mr Kiat How Tan, Senior Minister of State, Ministry of Digital Development and Information.

According to a 2025 MIT study on the GenAI Divide, 95% of enterprise AI initiatives fail to deliver measurable business value — leaving most organisations trapped between pilots and production. Our Centre of Excellence for Applied AI aims to change that to accelerate AI innovation and adoption and support their long-term AI ambitions.

Singtel Digital InfraCo CEO Bill Chang said, "We have seen many organisations struggle to move from the excitement of AI to meaningful impact. Through this Centre of Excellence with NVIDIA, we will provide access to world-class AI platforms, models, applications, expertise and training, helping enterprises harness AI effectively and responsibly."

Powered by NVIDIA's platforms, RE:AI (Singtel), Nxera and Singtel's advanced networks, the CoE will give enterprises and government agencies a hands-on environment to tackle their AI challenges, build MVPs and scale with confidence. We’ll be focusing on four areas: 

- Next-gen AI infrastructure – next-generation NVIDIA GPUs at 600KW to 1MW per rack densities 
- AI ecosystem – curating frontier models and solution providers for enterprise transformation 
- Edge AI – powering autonomous systems, robotics and embodied AI 
- AI talent – building deep AI skills and knowledge through R&D

We’re excited to announce that Nxera, Singtel Group’s regional data centre arm, today officially opened DC Tuas, Singapore’s highest power-density, most energy-efficient, and multi-tenanted data centre, purpose-built to support compute-intensive workloads and accelerate AI innovations.

“The opening of our new AI‑ready data centre, DC Tuas, is an important milestone for Nxera as we expand our role in enabling AI innovations in Singapore,” said Bill Chang, CEO of Nxera and Singtel’s Digital InfraCo. “The ability to deploy higher-density, compute-intensive AI workloads sustainably is increasingly critical for a strategic market where data centre capacity is constrained. As Singapore’s highest power-density, most energy-efficient and largest liquid-cooled multi-tenanted data centre, DC Tuas reinforces the country’s position as a global AI hub and supports accelerating AI adoption. It also demonstrates our expertise in delivering next-generation digital infrastructure and our abilities with Nxera in attracting and developing an exciting and diverse AI ecosystem for Singapore”

DC Tuas is built to the highest Green Mark Platinum standards and integrates smart thermal systems, high-efficiency electrical systems, and direct-to-chip liquid cooling to optimise performance in Singapore’s tropical climate. The facility also leverages solar power, rainwater harvesting, and water reuse systems, achieving a power usage effectiveness of 1.25, making it the most energy-efficient multi-tenanted data centre in Singapore and the region.

We are pleased to partner with KKR to acquire the remaining 82% stake in ST Telemedia Global Data Centres (STT GDC) for S$6.6 billion. The acquisition will give Singtel Group a 25% stake in an established high-quality, fast-growing data centre platform. 

Our Group CFO Arthur Lang 蓝道义 said, “This acquisition is a significant step towards scaling our new growth engine in digital infrastructure as mapped out in our Singtel28 growth plan. STT GDC's diverse geographical footprint increases our exposure to new markets and makes the Singtel Group a stronger data centre player with global reach. We appreciate ST Telemedia’s stewardship of the company and are confident that its seasoned leadership team will continue to scale the solid platform they have built. When added to our portfolio of data centre assets that includes Nxera in which KKR is also a capital partner, it meaningfully changes the business complexion of the Group while creating new opportunities for capital optimisation and growth. We will continue to exercise discipline in capital allocation and evaluate capital recycling alternatives to fund growth and maintain balance‑sheet efficiency. Our dividend and growth plans under Singtel28 remain intact.”

Singtel and KKR made our first investment in STT GDC back in June 2024. We see it reinforcing our unique capabilities across the full digital infrastructure value chain which includes our Nxera regional AI-ready data centres and RE:AI (Singtel) AI cloud service, strengthening our ability to capture opportunities from the accelerating demand for AI and cloud services. We look forward to working closely with KKR and STT GDC as we embark on this exciting new chapter together. Read more here: https://lnkd.in/gE_DNAam

#Singtel #Singtel28 #DigitalInfrastructure #DataCentres

Keeping Singapore safe depends on digital infrastructure that is reliable, trusted and built for national needs. For years, Singtel Group has invested early in connectivity, cybersecurity and sovereign digital infrastructure to support mission-critical operations and essential services Singapore relies on every day. 
 
Today, we renewed and expanded our Strategic Partnership for Innovation with HTX (Home Team Science & Technology Agency) for the next five years. Under the agreement, Singtel Singapore and Singtel Digital InfraCo will work closely with HTX to strengthen the resilience, performance and security of Home Team systems, through the development of next-generation technologies, including AI, network services and cybersecurity.
 
Singtel Singapore CEO Tian Chong Ng shared that “safeguarding Singapore requires staying ahead of fast-evolving technologies and increasingly sophisticated threats.” By bringing together HTX’s deep domain expertise and Singtel’s capabilities across connectivity, AI and security, the partnership equips Home Team officers with stronger tools, sharper insights and greater operational agility.

Singtel Digital InfraCo CEO Bill Chang highlighted the importance of trusted platforms, noting that “technology that is both trusted and sovereign is critical to safeguarding public security.” As part of the partnership, RE:AI (Singtel) — the AI cloud service of Singtel Digital InfraCo — will provide secure, high-performance AI infrastructure for public safety use cases, while keeping sensitive data within trusted local environments. 
 
As threats evolve, public safety demands infrastructure that performs at scale. Long-term investment in trusted digital capabilities helps ensure Singapore remains resilient and prepared for the future. 
 
#Singtel #HTX #PublicSafety #CyberSecurity #AI #SovereignAI

Singtel achieved strong results for FY26 with underlying net profit growing 12% to S$2.77 billion, driven mainly by our regional associates Airtel and AIS and operating companies NCS, Digital InfraCo and Optus. Net profit grew 40% to S$5.61 billion, boosted by a net exceptional gain of S$2.84 billion mainly from stake sales in Airtel. Operating revenue was stable at S$14.26 billion while both EBITDA and operating company EBIT rose 2% and 9% respectively due to the robust performances of NCS, Digital InfraCo and Optus.

Kuan Moon Yuen, Singtel Group CEO, said, “We delivered a strong set of results for the second year of our Singtel28 growth plan, which underscores the advantages of our diversified portfolio. Key to this was disciplined execution across connectivity, digital services and digital infrastructure which has been instrumental in the company achieving its medium-term ROIC target at 11.1%. With this improved performance and our active capital management, we are announcing our highest annual dividend of 18.5 cents to date.”

“The acquisition of STT GDC is a significant step forward in our journey to structurally shift our business towards digital infrastructure and services. In the coming year, our focus areas include leveraging the enhanced scale and strategic optionality from our STT GDC acquisition upon completion and capturing opportunities across the entire AI value chain, scaling Nxera and RE:AI, our GPU-as-a-service business, to meet sovereign and regional AI demand, and maintaining Singtel Singapore’s enterprise business growth momentum locally and internationally.”

 #Singtel #SingtelResults

We’re proud to be honoured at the National Trades Union Congress (NTUC) May Day Awards 2026, which celebrate organisations and individuals who make a meaningful difference for workers in Singapore.
 
🏅 Singtel was awarded the Plaque of Commendation (Star) for our sustained contributions to workers’ welfare and the Labour Movement – accepted by our Group CEO Kuan Moon Yuen on behalf of the company.
🏅 Moon was conferred the Medal of Commendation (Gold) – the highest honour for management personnel, in recognition of his leadership in advancing progressive wages, skills development, inclusive representation and employee well-being.
🏅 Our colleague Brother Mohamed Latiff Othman, President of the Union of Telecoms Employees of Singapore (UTES), was bestowed the Comrade of Labour Award for his dedication and advocacy as a union leader.
 
Receiving both awards at the ceremony, Moon said, “I am very honoured to receive these awards which are deeply meaningful – not just for Singtel as an organisation, but for every one of our employees who has leaned into change with courage and curiosity. These accolades affirm that we are on the right path in engaging and empowering our workforce to achieve our Singtel28 goals.”
 
Aileen Tan, Singtel’s Group Chief People and Sustainability Officer said, “We are deeply honoured to receive the Medal of Commendation (Star). and we accept it on behalf of every Singtel employee who chose to grow rather than stand still. In an AI-driven world, the real transformation is human – powered by the combined strengths of our people, technology and culture.”
 
These recognitions reflect the strength of tripartism and what is possible when companies, unions and workers progress together. At Singtel, we remain focused on supporting our people with the skills, opportunities and partnerships needed to grow, while continuing to build an inclusive, resilient and future-ready workplace.
 
Thank you to NTUC and our union partners for the continued trust and collaboration, and to colleagues across Singtel who inspire us every day to keep raising the bar.
 
Read more here: https://lnkd.in/gNPpZQeA

#Singtel #Singtel28 #MayDayAwards2026 #Tripartism

In emergencies, the speed and reliability of communication can make a critical difference — ensuring people receive clear, actionable information when it matters most.

Together with the Singapore Civil Defence Force, HTX (Home Team Science & Technology Agency) and IMDA, SG Alert, an emergency messaging system was launched to enable the rapid broadcast of critical information to the public during major incidents. The service employs cell broadcast technology to deliver alerts directly to mobile phones — even without mobile data. 

As the leading telco in Singapore, Singtel is the first mobile network operator to deploy this nationwide capability to our customers.

This service complements Singapore’s broader emergency communications framework, which includes platforms such as the Public Warning System, free-to-air television and radio broadcasts, social media, and the SGSecure app.

Singtel Singapore Chief Customer Officer (Enterprise) Keith Leong said, “In critical moments, information saves lives — and SG Alert enables urgent messages to reach large numbers of people simultaneously via their mobile phones through precise geo-targeting. This is one of many ways in which Singtel’s secure, scalable communications infrastructure and capabilities go beyond everyday connectivity to playing a vital role in safeguarding our communities and strengthening Singapore’s national resilience.”

Customers should note that the SCDF will be conducting an islandwide public test for Singtel network users on 10 May 2026. To ensure you receive these alerts, please update your mobile device to Apple iOS (26.4) or Android OS 12 by then. 
 
Read more here: https://lnkd.in/gvKciCca

#Singtel #SGAlert #PublicSafety #Resilience #Connectivity #DigitalInfrastructure #Singapore

Singtel Group has launched an exercise to transfer Singtel Special Discounted Shares (SDS) from the CPF Board to The Central Depository (CDP) accounts of SDS holders. This initiative benefits SDS holders by giving them greater ease and control in managing their holdings and exercise their rights as shareholders directly. 

Introduced in the 1990s, the SDS scheme was part of the government’s efforts to give Singaporeans a stake in Singapore’s economic success through share ownership. Many Singaporeans bought ST “A” shares in 1993 or ST2 shares in 1996 with their CPF savings and the CPF Board was appointed to facilitate the purchase as Singaporeans were new to share ownership at that time. Today, as Singaporeans are more knowledgeable and familiar with share ownership, the SDS scheme has met its intent and the legacy arrangement through CPF Board is no longer needed.

The transfer will enable SDS holders who hold shares in their individual CDP accounts to consolidate all their holdings, making them easier to track and trade. As Singtel shareholders, SDS holders will also benefit, as Singtel will have greater flexibility to carry out corporate actions in a timely and cost-efficient manner. This initiative will give Singtel more options to reward shareholders and fund growth initiatives. 

The transfer of Singtel SDS is scheduled on 21 November 2026, subject to legislative approval.

Enterprises have long treated backup data as a safeguard for recovery, but it holds far greater untapped value. Today, we announced Singtel Digital InfraCo’s sovereign AI cloud RE:AI (Singtel)’s partnership with Cohesity, to launch an intelligent, sovereign AI data security and management service in Singapore.
 
Many enterprises have years’ worth of important information such as old documents, emails, reports and system records stored away in backup systems. They are difficult for today’s large language models and AI applications to search or learn from as these AI tools are built to work only with current active data. The new offering from RE:AI and Cohesity empowers enterprises and government agencies to catalogue and organise vast amounts of historical data so it can be searched and queried in real time. This will help them to generate more accurate, data-driven responses from richer, longer-range context.
 
Singtel Digital InfraCo CEO Bill Chang said, "By combining RE:AI's sovereign AI capabilities with market-leading data security capabilities from Cohesity, enterprises can finally harness historical data that has long remained untapped to generate deeper insights and make faster, more informed decisions. Importantly, they can do this while keeping sensitive information protected and within Singapore’s regulatory environment."
 
This partnership also deepens Singtel Digital InfraCo’s AI ecosystem, with Cohesity contributing R&D resources to our new Centre of Excellence for Applied AI with NVIDIA.

We’re excited to announce that Nxera, Singtel Group’s regional data centre arm, today officially opened DC Tuas, Singapore’s highest power-density, most energy-efficient, and multi-tenanted data centre, purpose-built to support compute-intensive workloads and accelerate AI innovations.

“The opening of our new AI‑ready data centre, DC Tuas, is an important milestone for Nxera as we expand our role in enabling AI innovations in Singapore,” said Bill Chang, CEO of Nxera and Singtel’s Digital InfraCo. “The ability to deploy higher-density, compute-intensive AI workloads sustainably is increasingly critical for a strategic market where data centre capacity is constrained. As Singapore’s highest power-density, most energy-efficient and largest liquid-cooled multi-tenanted data centre, DC Tuas reinforces the country’s position as a global AI hub and supports accelerating AI adoption. It also demonstrates our expertise in delivering next-generation digital infrastructure and our abilities with Nxera in attracting and developing an exciting and diverse AI ecosystem for Singapore”

DC Tuas is built to the highest Green Mark Platinum standards and integrates smart thermal systems, high-efficiency electrical systems, and direct-to-chip liquid cooling to optimise performance in Singapore’s tropical climate. The facility also leverages solar power, rainwater harvesting, and water reuse systems, achieving a power usage effectiveness of 1.25, making it the most energy-efficient multi-tenanted data centre in Singapore and the region.

Keeping Singapore safe depends on digital infrastructure that is reliable, trusted and built for national needs. For years, Singtel Group has invested early in connectivity, cybersecurity and sovereign digital infrastructure to support mission-critical operations and essential services Singapore relies on every day. 
 
Today, we renewed and expanded our Strategic Partnership for Innovation with HTX (Home Team Science & Technology Agency) for the next five years. Under the agreement, Singtel Singapore and Singtel Digital InfraCo will work closely with HTX to strengthen the resilience, performance and security of Home Team systems, through the development of next-generation technologies, including AI, network services and cybersecurity.
 
Singtel Singapore CEO Tian Chong Ng shared that “safeguarding Singapore requires staying ahead of fast-evolving technologies and increasingly sophisticated threats.” By bringing together HTX’s deep domain expertise and Singtel’s capabilities across connectivity, AI and security, the partnership equips Home Team officers with stronger tools, sharper insights and greater operational agility.

Singtel Digital InfraCo CEO Bill Chang highlighted the importance of trusted platforms, noting that “technology that is both trusted and sovereign is critical to safeguarding public security.” As part of the partnership, RE:AI (Singtel) — the AI cloud service of Singtel Digital InfraCo — will provide secure, high-performance AI infrastructure for public safety use cases, while keeping sensitive data within trusted local environments. 
 
As threats evolve, public safety demands infrastructure that performs at scale. Long-term investment in trusted digital capabilities helps ensure Singapore remains resilient and prepared for the future.

We’re delighted to announce the renewal of Singtel Group’s partnership with National Gallery Singapore for the next decade, reaffirming our shared commitment to making art more accessible to all.
 
Singtel has been a Founding Partner since the Gallery’s opening in 2015, and the partnership has shaped Singapore’s cultural calendar with 16 exhibitions that brought internationally acclaimed art from Southeast Asia and the rest of the world to the doorsteps of Singaporeans. These include Yayoi Kusama’s first major exhibition in Southeast Asia, which drew a record crowd, and 'Tropical: Stories from Southeast Asia and Latin America' which broke ground in comparing the shared histories and influences across the two regions. Collectively, the Singtel Special Exhibition Gallery has welcomed 1.5 million visitors in the past decade.

Singtel Group CEO Kuan Moon Yuen shared, “We are extremely pleased to continue our support for National Gallery Singapore as it cements its place as one of the world’s leading art institutions. And in doing so, we’re reaffirming our commitment to inspiring audiences, deepening international relationships, and contributing to Singapore’s vibrant cultural landscape.”

The partnership renewal is aligned with Singtel’s longstanding commitment to backing the arts and our wider community. Through initiatives such as the annual Expressions Through Art exhibition, we have showcased hundreds of artworks by special education students to the public since 2022.

We are pleased to partner with KKR to acquire the remaining 82% stake in ST Telemedia Global Data Centres (STT GDC) for S$6.6 billion. The acquisition will give Singtel Group a 25% stake in an established high-quality, fast-growing data centre platform. 

Our Group CFO Arthur Lang 蓝道义 said, “This acquisition is a significant step towards scaling our new growth engine in digital infrastructure as mapped out in our Singtel28 growth plan. STT GDC's diverse geographical footprint increases our exposure to new markets and makes the Singtel Group a stronger data centre player with global reach. We appreciate ST Telemedia’s stewardship of the company and are confident that its seasoned leadership team will continue to scale the solid platform they have built. When added to our portfolio of data centre assets that includes Nxera in which KKR is also a capital partner, it meaningfully changes the business complexion of the Group while creating new opportunities for capital optimisation and growth. We will continue to exercise discipline in capital allocation and evaluate capital recycling alternatives to fund growth and maintain balance‑sheet efficiency. Our dividend and growth plans under Singtel28 remain intact.”

Singtel and KKR made our first investment in STT GDC back in June 2024. We see it reinforcing our unique capabilities across the full digital infrastructure value chain which includes our Nxera regional AI-ready data centres and RE:AI (Singtel) AI cloud service, strengthening our ability to capture opportunities from the accelerating demand for AI and cloud services. We look forward to working closely with KKR and STT GDC as we embark on this exciting new chapter together. Read more here: https://lnkd.in/gE_DNAam

Keeping Singapore safe depends on digital infrastructure that is reliable, trusted and built for national needs. For years, Singtel Group has invested early in connectivity, cybersecurity and sovereign digital infrastructure to support mission-critical operations and essential services Singapore relies on every day. 
 
Today, we renewed and expanded our Strategic Partnership for Innovation with HTX (Home Team Science & Technology Agency) for the next five years. Under the agreement, Singtel Singapore and Singtel Digital InfraCo will work closely with HTX to strengthen the resilience, performance and security of Home Team systems, through the development of next-generation technologies, including AI, network services and cybersecurity.
 
Singtel Singapore CEO Tian Chong Ng shared that “safeguarding Singapore requires staying ahead of fast-evolving technologies and increasingly sophisticated threats.” By bringing together HTX’s deep domain expertise and Singtel’s capabilities across connectivity, AI and security, the partnership equips Home Team officers with stronger tools, sharper insights and greater operational agility.

Singtel Digital InfraCo CEO Bill Chang highlighted the importance of trusted platforms, noting that “technology that is both trusted and sovereign is critical to safeguarding public security.” As part of the partnership, RE:AI (Singtel) — the AI cloud service of Singtel Digital InfraCo — will provide secure, high-performance AI infrastructure for public safety use cases, while keeping sensitive data within trusted local environments. 
 
As threats evolve, public safety demands infrastructure that performs at scale. Long-term investment in trusted digital capabilities helps ensure Singapore remains resilient and prepared for the future. 
 
#Singtel #HTX #PublicSafety #CyberSecurity #AI #SovereignAI

If you’re headed to town this weekend, do drop by the Singtel Expressions Through Art 2025 exhibition at ION! Previously hosted at Gardens by the Bay, the exhibition is now open to the public at the ION Art Gallery at Level 4 until 1 February. 🎨💛
 
Inspired by SG60, the exhibition is based on the theme “My Singapore, My Home”. We’re delighted to announce the renewal of Singtel Group’s partnership with National Gallery Singapore for the next decade, reaffirming our shared commitment to making art more accessible to all.
 
Singtel has been a Founding Partner since the Gallery’s opening in 2015, and the partnership has shaped Singapore’s cultural calendar with 16 exhibitions that brought internationally acclaimed art from Southeast Asia and the rest of the world to the doorsteps of Singaporeans. These include Yayoi Kusama’s first major exhibition in Southeast Asia, which drew a record crowd, and 'Tropical: Stories from Southeast Asia and Latin America' which broke ground in comparing the shared histories and influences across the two regions. Collectively, the Singtel Special Exhibition Gallery has welcomed 1.5 million visitors in the past decade.

Singtel Group CEO Kuan Moon Yuen shared, “We are extremely pleased to continue our support for National Gallery Singapore as it cements its place as one of the world’s leading art institutions. And in doing so, we’re reaffirming our commitment to inspiring audiences, deepening international relationships, and contributing to Singapore’s vibrant cultural landscape.”

The partnership renewal is aligned with Singtel’s longstanding commitment to backing the arts and our wider community. Through initiatives such as the annual Expressions Through Art exhibition, we have showcased hundreds of artworks by special education students to the public since 2022.

#Singtel #NationalGallerySingapore #ArtsAndCulture #Partnerships #Community

From AI promise to realising AI’s potential – today, Singtel Digital InfraCo and NVIDIA announced the launch of a Centre of Excellence for Applied AI in Singapore at an event graced by Guest of Honour Mr Kiat How Tan, Senior Minister of State, Ministry of Digital Development and Information.

According to a 2025 MIT study on the GenAI Divide, 95% of enterprise AI initiatives fail to deliver measurable business value — leaving most organisations trapped between pilots and production. Our Centre of Excellence for Applied AI aims to change that to accelerate AI innovation and adoption and support their long-term AI ambitions.

Singtel Digital InfraCo CEO Bill Chang said, "We have seen many organisations struggle to move from the excitement of AI to meaningful impact. Through this Centre of Excellence with NVIDIA, we will provide access to world-class AI platforms, models, applications, expertise and training, helping enterprises harness AI effectively and responsibly."

Powered by NVIDIA's platforms, RE:AI (Singtel), Nxera and Singtel's advanced networks, the CoE will give enterprises and government agencies a hands-on environment to tackle their AI challenges, build MVPs and scale with confidence. We’ll be focusing on four areas: 

- Next-gen AI infrastructure – next-generation NVIDIA GPUs at 600KW to 1MW per rack densities 
- AI ecosystem – curating frontier models and solution providers for enterprise transformation 
- Edge AI – powering autonomous systems, robotics and embodied AI 
- AI talent – building deep AI skills and knowledge through R&D

We’re excited to announce that Nxera, Singtel Group’s regional data centre arm, today officially opened DC Tuas, Singapore’s highest power-density, most energy-efficient, and multi-tenanted data centre, purpose-built to support compute-intensive workloads and accelerate AI innovations.

“The opening of our new AI‑ready data centre, DC Tuas, is an important milestone for Nxera as we expand our role in enabling AI innovations in Singapore,” said Bill Chang, CEO of Nxera and Singtel’s Digital InfraCo. “The ability to deploy higher-density, compute-intensive AI workloads sustainably is increasingly critical for a strategic market where data centre capacity is constrained. As Singapore’s highest power-density, most energy-efficient and largest liquid-cooled multi-tenanted data centre, DC Tuas reinforces the country’s position as a global AI hub and supports accelerating AI adoption. It also demonstrates our expertise in delivering next-generation digital infrastructure and our abilities with Nxera in attracting and developing an exciting and diverse AI ecosystem for Singapore”

DC Tuas is built to the highest Green Mark Platinum standards and integrates smart thermal systems, high-efficiency electrical systems, and direct-to-chip liquid cooling to optimise performance in Singapore’s tropical climate. The facility also leverages solar power, rainwater harvesting, and water reuse systems, achieving a power usage effectiveness of 1.25, making it the most energy-efficient multi-tenanted data centre in Singapore and the region.

We recently conducted an e-waste collection drive and are happy to share that we collected over 828kg! 
 
Part of Singtel’s dematerialisation strategy, the initiative involved 36 Singtel volunteers and 32 passionate students from Nanyang Polytechnic going door-to-door across Cheng San Cactus Sunrise Estate. Together, we engaged with residents from over 1,000 homes to raise awareness of the importance of e-waste recycling and refurbishment. We also collected a range of e-waste like electric fans, cables, TVs and kitchen appliances. 
 
These items will be responsibly recycled by our partner ALBA Group Asia, while myhalo.sg will refurbish suitable devices for donation to families in need. 
 
We even had Nadia Ahmad Samdin, Adviser to Ang Mo Kio GRC GROs pop by to greet the volunteers! 

This initiative supports the national e-waste recycling programme and is in line with Singtel’s commitment to building a sustainable future. 

If you’d like to join us in our e-waste management efforts, you can:
 - Donate your old devices at our device donation bins across Singtel shops upon your visit 
 - Hand over your e-waste to our Singtel field engineers when they visit your homes for installations
 
Alternatively, you may drop off your e-waste into any of the 1,000 ALBA bins located island wide.
 
To find out more about Singtel’s e-waste management initiatives and other sustainability efforts, please visit https://lnkd.in/g78xTXcB or https://lnkd.in/gTdhPvmg

Singtel has been a Founding Partner since the Gallery’s opening in 2015, and the partnership has shaped Singapore’s cultural calendar with 16 exhibitions that brought internationally acclaimed art from Southeast Asia and the rest of the world to the doorsteps of Singaporeans. These include Yayoi Kusama’s first major exhibition in Southeast Asia, which drew a record crowd, and 'Tropical: Stories from Southeast Asia and Latin America' which broke ground in comparing the shared histories and influences across the two regions. Collectively, the Singtel Special Exhibition Gallery has welcomed 1.5 million visitors in the past decade.

Singtel Group CEO Kuan Moon Yuen shared, “We are extremely pleased to continue our support for National Gallery Singapore as it cements its place as one of the world’s leading art institutions. And in doing so, we’re reaffirming our commitment to inspiring audiences, deepening international relationships, and contributing to Singapore’s vibrant cultural landscape.”

The partnership renewal is aligned with Singtel’s longstanding commitment to backing the arts and our wider community. Through initiatives such as the annual Expressions Through Art exhibition, we have showcased hundreds of artworks by special education students to the public since 2022.

Paragon also helps telcos to monetise network APIs and build sovereign AI factories within their own countries. It is gratifying to be recognised by Frost & Sullivan for the strength and scale of our AI infrastructure, orchestration capabilities and partner ecosystem as well as unwavering commitment to customers.”

We recently conducted an e-waste collection drive and are happy to share that we collected over 828kg! 
 
Part of Singtel’s dematerialisation strategy, the initiative involved 36 Singtel volunteers and 32 passionate students from Nanyang Polytechnic going door-to-door across Cheng San Cactus Sunrise Estate. Together, we engaged with residents from over 1,000 homes to raise awareness of the importance of e-waste recycling and refurbishment. We also collected a range of e-waste like electric fans, cables, TVs and kitchen appliances. 
 
These items will be responsibly recycled by our partner ALBA Group Asia, while myhalo.sg will refurbish suitable devices for donation to families in need. 
 
We even had Nadia Ahmad Samdin, Adviser to Ang Mo Kio GRC GROs pop by to greet the volunteers! 

This initiative supports the national e-waste recycling programme and is in line with Singtel’s commitment to building a sustainable future. 

If you’d like to join us in our e-waste management efforts, you can:
 - Donate your old devices at our device donation bins across Singtel shops upon your visit 
 - Hand over your e-waste to our Singtel field engineers when they visit your homes for installations
 
Alternatively, you may drop off your e-waste into any of the 1,000 ALBA bins located island wide.
 
To find out more about Singtel’s e-waste management initiatives and other sustainability efforts, please visit https://lnkd.in/g78xTXcB or https://lnkd.in/gTdhPvmg

The exhibition Into the Modern: Impressionism from the Museum of Fine Arts, Boston is now open at the Singtel Special Exhibition Gallery at National Gallery Singapore — and we couldn’t be more thrilled to bring these iconic works to our shores. The launch also kickstarts a line-up of programmes marking the Gallery’s 10th anniversary.

Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth David Neo graced the opening of the exhibition and underscored the important role that corporate partners play in supporting the arts, noting Singtel’s decade-long partnership with the Gallery, “We appreciate this partnership with Singtel to bring art to new audiences and connect them through art.” He also added, “When we are working together, we are better together, and we are stronger together.”

Singtel has proudly supported National Gallery Singapore since 2015, and advocacy for the arts has been a cornerstone of our commitment to the community. Over the past decade, the Singtel Special Exhibition Gallery has welcomed more than 1.5 million visitors — a milestone that reflects our dedication to making world-class art accessible to all.

Lian Pek, Singtel’s Vice President of Group Corporate Affairs and Investor Relations, shared, “We are immensely pleased to support this rare impressionism showcase at the Singtel Special Exhibition Gallery which drew more than 10,000 visitors on its opening weekend! Who would have thought the iconic masterpieces of Monet, Cezanne and Degas would virtually be on the doorsteps of Singaporeans and accessible to everyone visiting our little red dot? With the exhibition marking our decade of support for the gallery and its 10th anniversary, we are very proud to be associated with a national institution that is connecting communities and facilitating conversations about different and diverse ways of seeing the world.”

Into the Modern is the largest French Impressionism exhibition ever presented in Southeast Asia, featuring masterpieces on loan from MFA Boston. The exhibition runs through 1 March 2026.

Singtel delivered a strong first-half growth for FY26. Underlying net profit – from which the Group’s core dividends are based – rose 14% to S$1.35 billion, and would have increased 22% in constant currency terms, driven largely by robust performances from Optus, NCS and regional associates Airtel and AIS. Net profit rose to S$3.40 billion, boosted by a net exception gain of S$2.05 billion mainly from the sale of a partial stake in Airtel in May and the Intouch-Gulf merger. Operating revenue was down 1.2% due to the strong Singapore dollar and would have risen 1.9% in constant currency terms, while operating company EBIT grew 13%.

Kuan Moon Yuen, Singtel Group CEO, said, “The Group's first half results reflect the positive momentum across our diversified portfolio of businesses across the region. We continued to drive growth in connectivity, digital services and digital infrastructure and also unlocked more value from our asset recycling efforts as we executed to our Singtel28 plan. While the macroeconomic outlook remains challenging, and the Optus business faces uncertainty, our business and geographical diversity is lending stability to the Group’s performance.” He added, “We expect our growth engines to change the complexion of the business in the mid term as they continue to scale. Nxera's EBITDA should achieve a more than 20% compound annual growth rate over the next four years as it progressively adds new operational data centre capacity. We also see NCS keeping up its business momentum on robust bookings.”

Since the start of the Singtel28 plan, the Group’s active capital management has generated S$5.6 billion in proceeds including S$1.5 billion from its divestment of a 0.8% stake in Airtel last week. The Group has achieved more than half of its new S$9 billion mid-term asset recycling target which will be used to fund growth opportunities and returns to shareholders.

Singtel’s Board has approved an interim ordinary dividend of 8.2 cents per share, up 17%. This comprises a core dividend of 6.4 cents per share and a value realisation dividend of 1.8 cents per share, totalling S$1.35 billion.

🎨 We launched the Expressions Through Art 2025 at Gardens by the Bay today, showcasing 25 artworks by students from all 25 special education schools in Singapore. 

Students expressed their perspectives on what Singapore means to them and how they imagine its future with scenes such as the country’s iconic landmarks, neighbourhood playgrounds, public housing blocks and train stations. Some also used their art to explore themes such as their cultural heritage and the Singaporean ‘kampung spirit’. 

Aileen Tan, Group Chief People and Sustainability Officer at Singtel said, “Our goal has been to give students with special needs a platform to express their creativity and imagination. This is an integral part of our efforts to help them to realise their full potential, and of our longstanding support of the local arts scene. As Expressions Through Art enters its fourth year, we’re delighted that it has nurtured confidence and sparked connections. Each artwork for SG60 is a window to how they see their world and dream it could be, conveying unique stories of hope and belonging. These young artists show us that creativity knows no boundaries, and that every individual can inspire and enrich our society. We’re honoured to celebrate their talents and share their art with the wider community.”

Catch the exhibition from now until 21 November 2025 at the Gardens by the Bay’s North Colonnade area, near the Supertree Grove. You can also look out for digitised versions at digital screens within Parkway Parade, 313@Somerset and JEM; on the Gardens by the Bay website; as well as at selected train stations and bus shelters near SPED schools island-wide to reach a wider audience.

For the second consecutive year, Singtel is proud to sponsor the Her World Singapore Hello Possibilities Award, which recognises women who are breaking barriers in science, technology, and innovation.

Presented by our Group Chief People and Sustainability Officer Aileen Tan at the Her World Woman of the Year 2025 Gala, the award celebrates the courage and curiosity that fuel progress in a fast-changing world.

At the event held last night, Aileen shared, “Being fearless doesn’t mean having no fear — it means feeling it and choosing to move forward anyway. We call it being future fearless: showing up each day with courage, curiosity and heart, daring to experiment, fail, learn and try again — while keeping people and purpose at the centre of everything we do.”

This year’s Hello Possibilities Award honourees — Space Faculty’s Lynette Tan, who has been instrumental in pioneering Singapore’s space sector; Dr Malika Meghjani, Assistant Professor at Singapore University of Technology and Design (SUTD) and a driving force in robotics and STEM education; and Dr. Ayesha Khanna, Co-Founder and CEO of artificial intelligence advisory and incubator Addo AI — exemplify that spirit. Their work in advancing Singapore’s digital future inspires us all. 

We also congratulate Bengawan Solo’s Anastasia Tjendri-Liew, Her World Woman of the Year 2025 for transforming Bengawan Solo from a humble home-based kueh business into one of Singapore’s most beloved heritage brands, all while preserving its artisanal traditions and championing local craftsmanship, and pro bono lawyer Sadhana Rai, Young Woman of the Year for her tireless advocacy for underprivileged communities and her work providing free legal aid to migrant workers, survivors of abuse, and others without a voice.

Representation for women in tech matters and we’re proud to continue shining a spotlight on the women who are making a difference. Learn more about how we’re empowering our people and driving inclusion in our latest sustainability report: https://lnkd.in/g_acu4Jn

#Singtel #WomenInSTEM #HelloPossibilities #HerWorldWomanOfTheYear #DiversityAndInclusion #FutureFearless

We also congratulate Bengawan Solo’s Anastasia Tjendri-Liew, Her World Woman of the Year 2025 for transforming Bengawan Solo from a humble home-based kueh business into one of Singapore’s most beloved heritage brands, all while preserving its artisanal traditions and championing local craftsmanship, and pro bono lawyer Sadhana Rai, Young Woman of the Year for her tireless advocacy for underprivileged communities and her work providing free legal aid to migrant workers, survivors of abuse, and others without a voice.

Representation for women in tech matters and we’re proud to continue shining a spotlight on the women who are making a difference. Learn more about how we’re empowering our people and driving inclusion in our latest sustainability report: https://lnkd.in/g_acu4Jn

#Singtel #WomenInSTEM #HelloPossibilities #HerWorldWomanOfTheYear #DiversityAndInclusion #FutureFearless

Say HELLO to Swasti Arya! 👋

A Computer Science & Design student from Singapore University of Technology and Design (SUTD), Swasti joined Singtel as a Process Automation Executive under the Management Associate Programme Internship, driven by a passion to learn how technology and business create real impact.

One of her proudest moments? Automating a manual process, cutting the time from 12 hours to just 1! The solution allowed her teammates to focus on more meaningful work.

“It was incredibly fulfilling to see how my work contributed to something bigger!” Swasti shares. “Thanks to the team’s open culture and willingness to embrace new ideas, I discovered countless opportunities to add value.”

At Singtel, we empower our interns to step out of their comfort zone and say hello to big possibilities — because the impact you can make is limitless.

Don't miss your chance to join us! Applications for our graduate programme and summer internship close on 7 Nov.
